content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 28 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic)
**Leningrad Codex:** Folios 224–227 | **Vaticanus:** pp. 159–161
**Chapter:** 69 verses (including v.69 in Hebrew = colophon)

---

**1** "And if you diligently listen to the voice of the LORD your God, being careful to do all his commandments that I am commanding you today, the LORD your God will set you high above all the nations of the earth.

**2** And all these blessings shall come upon you and overtake you, if you obey the voice of the LORD your God:

**3** Blessed shall you be in the city, and blessed shall you be in the field.

**4** Blessed shall be the fruit of your womb, and the fruit of your ground, and the fruit of your livestock — the offspring of your cattle and the young of your flock.

**5** Blessed shall be your basket and your kneading bowl.

**6** Blessed shall you be when you come in, and blessed shall you be when you go out.

**7** The LORD will cause your enemies who rise against you to be defeated before you. They shall come out against you one way and flee before you seven ways.

**8** The LORD will command the blessing upon you in your storehouses and in all that you undertake. And he will bless you in the land that the LORD your God is giving you.

**9** The LORD will establish you as a holy people for himself, as he has sworn to you, if you keep the commandments of the LORD your God and walk in his ways.

**10** And all the peoples of the earth shall see that you are called by the name of the LORD, and they shall be in awe of you.

**11** And the LORD will make you abound in prosperity, in the fruit of your womb and in the fruit of your livestock and in the fruit of your ground, within the land that the LORD swore to your fathers to give you.

**12** The LORD will open to you his good treasury, the heavens, to give the rain of your land in its season and to bless all the work of your hands. And you shall lend to many nations, but you shall not borrow.

**13** And the LORD will make you the head and not the tail, and you shall only go up and not down, if you obey the commandments of the LORD your God that I am commanding you today, being careful to do them,

**14** and if you do not turn aside from any of the words that I am commanding you today, to the right hand or to the left, to walk after other gods to serve them.

**15** But if you do not obey the voice of the LORD your God or be careful to do all his commandments and his statutes that I am commanding you today, all these curses shall come upon you and overtake you:

**16** Cursed shall you be in the city, and cursed shall you be in the field.

**17** Cursed shall be your basket and your kneading bowl.

**18** Cursed shall be the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your ground, the offspring of your cattle and the young of your flock.

**19** Cursed shall you be when you come in, and cursed shall you be when you go out.

**20** The LORD will send upon you the curse [me'erah], the panic, and the rebuke in all that you undertake to do, until you are destroyed and perish quickly, on account of the evil of your deeds by which you have forsaken me.

**21** The LORD will make the pestilence cling to you until he has consumed you from the land that you are entering to possess.

**22** The LORD will strike you with wasting disease and with fever and with inflammation and with fiery heat and with drought and with blight and with mildew; and they shall pursue you until you perish.

**23** And your heavens over your head shall be bronze, and the earth beneath you shall be iron.

**24** The LORD will make the rain of your land powder and dust; from the heavens it shall come down upon you until you are destroyed.

**25** The LORD will cause you to be defeated before your enemies. You shall go out against them one way and flee before them seven ways; and you shall be a horror to all the kingdoms of the earth.

**26** And your carcass shall be food for every bird of the heavens and for the beast of the earth, and there shall be no one to frighten them away.

**27** The LORD will strike you with the boils of Egypt, and with tumors, and with scurvy, and with itch, from which you cannot be healed.

**28** The LORD will strike you with madness and with blindness and with confusion of heart,

**29** and you shall grope at midday as the blind man gropes in darkness, and you shall not prosper in your ways; and you shall be only oppressed and robbed continually, and there shall be no one to help you.

**30** You shall betroth a wife, but another man shall lie with her. You shall build a house, but you shall not dwell in it. You shall plant a vineyard, but you shall not enjoy its fruit.

**31** Your ox shall be slaughtered before your eyes, but you shall not eat of it. Your donkey shall be seized in front of you and shall not be returned to you. Your flock shall be given to your enemies, and there shall be no one to help you.

**32** Your sons and your daughters shall be given to another people, while your eyes look on and fail with longing for them all day long, but you shall be helpless.

**33** A nation that you have not known shall eat up the fruit of your ground and all your labor; and you shall be only oppressed and crushed continually,

**34** so that you are driven mad by the sight of your eyes that you see.

**35** The LORD will strike you on the knees and on the legs with severe boils from which you cannot be healed, from the sole of your foot to the crown of your head.

**36** The LORD will bring you and your king whom you set over you to a nation that neither you nor your fathers have known; and there you shall serve other gods — wood and stone.

**37** And you shall become a horror, a proverb, and a byword among all the peoples to whom the LORD will lead you away.

**38** You shall carry much seed out to the field, but you shall gather in little, for the locust shall consume it.

**39** You shall plant vineyards and tend them, but you shall neither drink the wine nor gather the grapes, for the worm shall eat them.

**40** You shall have olive trees throughout all your territory, but you shall not anoint yourself with the oil, for your olives shall drop off.

**41** You shall father sons and daughters, but they shall not be yours, for they shall go into captivity.

**42** The locust shall take over all your trees and the fruit of your ground.

**43** The sojourner who is in your midst shall rise higher and higher above you, and you shall go down lower and lower.

**44** He shall lend to you, but you shall not lend to him. He shall be the head, and you shall be the tail.

**45** And all these curses shall come upon you and pursue you and overtake you until you are destroyed, because you did not obey the voice of the LORD your God, to keep his commandments and his statutes that he commanded you.

**46** They shall be a sign and a wonder against you and your offspring forever.

**47** Because you did not serve the LORD your God with joyfulness and gladness of heart, because of the abundance of all things —

**48** therefore you shall serve your enemies whom the LORD will send against you, in hunger and in thirst and in nakedness and in lack of everything; and he will put an iron yoke upon your neck until he has destroyed you.

**49** The LORD will bring a nation against you from far away, from the end of the earth, swooping down like an eagle — a nation whose language you do not understand —

**50** a fierce-looking nation who does not respect the old or show favor to the young.

**51** It shall eat up the offspring of your livestock and the fruit of your ground, until you are destroyed. It shall not leave you grain, new wine, or oil, the offspring of your cattle or the young of your flock, until they have caused you to perish.

**52** They shall besiege you within all your towns, until your high and fortified walls, in which you trusted, come down throughout all your land; and they shall besiege you within all your towns throughout all your land that the LORD your God has given you.

**53** And you shall eat the fruit of your womb — the flesh of your sons and your daughters, whom the LORD your God has given you — in the siege and in the distress with which your enemy shall distress you.

**54** The man among you who is most tender and most refined shall look grudgingly upon his brother and upon the wife of his bosom and upon the last of his children whom he still has remaining —

**55** so that he will not give to any of them any of the flesh of his children that he is eating, because there is nothing left to him in the siege and in the distress with which your enemy shall distress you within all your towns.

**56** The most tender and refined woman among you — who would not venture to set the sole of her foot upon the ground because of her delicacy and tenderness — shall look grudgingly upon the husband of her bosom and upon her son and upon her daughter,

**57** and upon her afterbirth that comes out from between her feet and upon her children whom she bears, for she shall eat them in secret for lack of everything, in the siege and in the distress with which your enemy shall distress you within your towns.

**58** If you are not careful to do all the words of this instruction that are written in this book, that you may fear this glorious and awesome name — the LORD your God —

**59** then the LORD will bring on you and your offspring extraordinary plagues, plagues severe and lasting, and sicknesses grievous and lasting.

**60** And he will bring back upon you all the diseases of Egypt, of which you were afraid, and they shall cling to you.

**61** Every sickness also and every plague that is not recorded in the book of this instruction, the LORD will bring upon you, until you are destroyed.

**62** And you shall be left few in number, whereas you were as numerous as the stars of the heavens, because you did not obey the voice of the LORD your God.

**63** And just as the LORD rejoiced over you to do you good and to multiply you, so the LORD will rejoice over you to bring ruin upon you and to destroy you. And you shall be plucked off the land that you are entering to possess.

**64** And the LORD will scatter you among all the peoples, from one end of the earth to the other end of the earth; and there you shall serve other gods that you have not known — you nor your fathers — wood and stone.

**65** And among those nations you shall find no rest, and there shall be no resting place for the sole of your foot; but the LORD will give you there a trembling heart and failing eyes and a languishing soul.

**66** And your life shall hang in suspense before you; and you shall be in dread night and day, and you shall have no assurance of your life.

**67** In the morning you shall say: "If only it were evening!" and in the evening you shall say: "If only it were morning!" — because of the dread of your heart that you dread and the sight of your eyes that you see.

**68** And the LORD will bring you back to Egypt in ships, by the road of which I said to you: "You shall never see it again"; and there you shall offer yourselves for sale to your enemies as male and female slaves, but there shall be no buyer.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**Structure — The tochechah:** All three agents note the great asymmetry: 14 verses of blessing (vv.1–14), 54 verses of curse (vv.15–68). The deliberate literary imbalance is itself a theological warning. Consensus preserves every verse without compression.

**V.20 — me'erah:** EMET preserves the Hebrew term me'erah (curse/blight) with brackets. LOGOS: "curses, confusion, and frustration." PNEUMA: "curses, confusion, and frustration." The Hebrew is me'erah, mehumah, migeret — three distinct nouns. Consensus preserves the bracketed [me'erah] for the first term, as EMET does, since it is a technical covenant term.

**V.22 — cherev as sword/drought:** EMET notes the textual ambiguity (cherev vs. charavah). LOGOS and PNEUMA render "drought." Consensus renders "drought" in keeping with the agricultural context and LOGOS/PNEUMA agreement.

**V.30 — "Ravish" vs. "lie with":** PNEUMA: "rape her" for the Hebrew yishkavennah. LOGOS: "ravish her." EMET: "lie with her." The Hebrew is simply "shall lie with her." In this context of enemy conquest the violation is implied. Consensus: "shall lie with her" — the Hebrew does not add explicit force language; context conveys it.

**V.42 — tselatsal (cicada/locust/cricket):** EMET: "cicada [tselatsal]"; LOGOS: "cricket"; PNEUMA: "swarms of locusts." The Hebrew tselatsal is disputed (cicada or locust type). Consensus: "locust" — most familiar term, acknowledged in EMET's bracketed note.

**V.63 — "Rejoice" over destruction:** All three agents preserve the striking use of the same verb (sus, "rejoice") for God's joy over blessing (vv.11, 63) and over destruction (v.63). This theological difficulty is preserved in the consensus without softening.

**V.68 — "In ships":** All three agents note this striking detail. Consensus retains "in ships" exactly.

**V.69 (Hebrew) / Colophon:** LOGOS includes the verse "These are the words of the covenant that the LORD commanded Moses to make with the sons of Israel in the land of Moab, besides the covenant that he had made with them at Horeb." EMET and PNEUMA do not include this as a verse of ch.28. Per LOGOS's note, the Leningrad Codex places this as the last verse of chapter 28. However, since EMET follows Hebrew versification and includes it in chapter 29 (as 29:1), and per project convention the chapters follow their own agent structures, this verse is placed in the DEU_029 consensus as its opening verse, consistent with EMET's versification.

— SOPHIA

---

*Sources: EMET (DEU_028), LOGOS (DEU_028), PNEUMA (DEU_028)*
